heading: Use of Army, Navy, Marine Corps, Air Force, and Space Force as posse comitatus
label: 1385 Use of Army, Navy, Marine Corps, Air Force, and Space Force as posse comitatus
text: Whoever, except in cases and under circumstances expressly authorized by the Constitution or Act of Congress, willfully uses any part of the Army, the Navy, the Marine Corps, the Air Force, or the Space Force as a posse comitatus or otherwise to execute the laws shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than two years, or both.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised SectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)138510:15., ; (proviso); added (less last proviso), .June 18, 1878, ch. 263, § 1520 Stat. 152Mar. 3, 1899, ch. 429, § 363June 6, 1900, ch. 786, § 2931 Stat. 330This section is revised to conform to the style and terminology used in title 18. It is not enacted as a part of title 10, United States Code, since it is more properly allocated to title 18.

Amendments2021— substituted “Army, Navy, Marine Corps, Air Force, and Space Force” for “Army and Air Force” in section catchline, and in text substituted “Army, the Navy, the Marine Corps,” for “Army or” and inserted “, or the Space Force” after “Air Force”.Pub. L. 117–811994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $10,000”.Pub. L. 103–3221959— struck out provisions which made section inapplicable in Alaska.Pub. L. 86–70
